context.projectOverview: Ports the Claude Agent SDK agent-loop capabilities into agent-bober's own provider-agnostic loop as additive, default-off changes. Sprints 1-4 implement the four architecture-backed high-value gaps (refusal detection, cost substrate + USD budget ceiling, per-role effort, parallel read-only tools); sprints 5-10 implement the remaining capability areas (loop events + hooks, sessions/resume/fork, in-context compaction, streaming deltas, mid-turn interrupt, in-process subagents + opt-in MCP bridge) in the own-loop idiom per explicit user direction, bound by the same hard constraints.

completedSprints.title: Refusal detection end-to-end
completedSprints.summary: 35a2dbd: 'refusal' StopReason in adapters, refused:true on AgenticLoopResult, fail-closed parseGeneratorResult.

completedSprints.title: Cost substrate
completedSprints.summary: 8d68248..73053c0: cost-meter.ts PRICE_TABLE, ChatResponse.costUsd all adapters, Budget maxUsd axis.

completedSprints.title: Loop wiring: effort, USD ceiling, config, cost persistence
completedSprints.summary: b9c936c: EffortSchema+BudgetSectionSchema on 4 role sections; AgenticLoopParams {effort?, budget?}; per-turn charging + graceful budget_exceeded return; AgenticLoopResult.costUsd on all 4 return sites; sprint-passed history event carries optional costUsd; generator wired via budgetFromMaxUsd. NOTE: agentic-loop.ts line numbers have shifted again — the serial tool-execution block and per-tool catch must be re-located by pattern. Suite 3770.

contract.title: Parallel read-only tool execution (ToolDef.readOnly + executeToolBatch + per-role flag)
contract.description: Replace the loop's strictly serial tool execution with a delegating executor that runs contiguous runs of read-only-annotated tool calls concurrently while everything else stays serial. Classification is a ToolDef.readOnly annotation (ADR-2, conservative allow-list: read_file/glob/grep only — bash never). Results preserve original order, per-tool failures become in-slot error results, and the batch never rejects. Enabled by a per-role parallelReadOnlyTools config flag, default false → byte-identical serial semantics.

successCriteria.description: ToolDef supports optional readOnly?: boolean; exactly read_file, glob and grep are annotated readOnly:true in the tool schemas; bash, write_file and edit_file are unmarked.

With parallel enabled, a turn requesting multiple read-only tools executes them concurrently — proven by delayed fake handlers where total elapsed is less than the sum of individual delays — and the returned ToolResult[] preserves the original tool-call order by toolUseId.

A throwing handler or unknown tool inside a parallel batch produces an isError ToolResult in its original slot with the same shape as the serial path's error results, and executeToolBatch resolves (never rejects).
With the flag off, or for any unmarked tool, execution order and results are byte-identical to the pre-change serial for-await loop (all existing loop/tool tests pass unchanged).
A per-role parallelReadOnlyTools boolean config field (optional, default false) reaches AgenticLoopParams; existing configs parse unchanged.
npm run build and npm run typecheck pass.

nonGoals: Do not annotate bash as read-only under any circumstance (a shell command can mutate state).
Do not parallelize write tools or mixed batches beyond contiguous read-only runs.
Do not hard-code a tool-name allow-list inside the loop (ADR-2: classification travels with the ToolDef).
Do not enable the flag by default anywhere.
stopConditions: npm run build passes and tests assert: annotation coverage, proven concurrency with order preservation, in-slot error containment without batch rejection, byte-identical serial fallback, and the config flag path.
contract.definitionOfDone: Tool execution is delegated to executeToolBatch; with the per-role flag on, independent read-only calls overlap in time while results keep their original order and failures stay in-slot; with the flag off or annotations absent, behavior is byte-identical to the serial loop. Build and full suite green.
assumptions: The serial loop lives at src/orchestrator/agentic-loop.ts ~:353-388 with the unknown-tool error path (~:362-369) and per-tool catch (~:379-387) whose result shapes the executor must mirror exactly.
Tool schemas live in src/orchestrator/tools/schemas.ts (~:9-162) and handlers in src/orchestrator/tools/handlers.ts (ToolHandler ~:21-23).
graph_* tools (src/graph/prompts.ts context) are NOT annotated this sprint — they can opt in later per ADR-2's self-declaration model.
onToolUse callback timing may shift for parallel batches (fired at dispatch); existing tests observe presence/args, not strict wall-clock interleaving.
outOfScope: Parallelizing MCP-bridged tools (sprint 10 tools default to serial via absent annotation).
Cross-turn pipelining or speculative tool execution.
contract.generatorNotes: Create src/orchestrator/tools/executor.ts exporting executeToolBatch(batch: { toolCalls, toolHandlers, readOnlyTools: Set<string>, parallel: boolean, onToolUse? }): Promise<ToolResult[]>. Algorithm: walk toolCalls grouping maximal contiguous runs where parallel && readOnlyTools.has(name); execute such runs with Promise.all over wrapped handlers (each wrapper try/catches into the exact isError ToolResult shape used by the serial catch), everything else strictly sequential; assemble results indexed by position so order is preserved. Add readOnly?: boolean to the ToolDef type (schemas.ts) and annotate read_file/glob/grep only. In agentic-loop.ts, derive readOnlyTools once from params.tools and replace the ~:353-388 block with a call to executeToolBatch (parallel = params.parallelReadOnlyTools === true); keep the ToolResultMessage append (~:392) unchanged. Add the per-role Zod flag next to sprint 3's effort/budget fields (coordinate: if sprint 3 already landed, extend the same section; if building independently, add just this flag additively) and thread it from the generator entry point. Use fake handlers with setTimeout delays in tests; measure elapsed with performance.now().
contract.evaluatorNotes: sc-4-1: assert schemas' readOnly flags exactly (three true, bash/write_file/edit_file undefined). sc-4-2: three fake read-only handlers each delayed ~50ms, parallel=true → elapsed < 120ms (vs ~150ms serial) AND results array matches input order with correct toolUseIds; also verify a serial run of the same batch takes >= 140ms to prove the fixture is meaningful. sc-4-3: middle handler throws / middle tool unknown → its slot isError with message shape matching the serial path (compare against a serial-path fixture), other slots normal, promise resolves. sc-4-4: parallel=false and unmarked-tool batches → assert handler invocation order equals input order (record call order); run FULL existing suite. sc-4-5: config fixture without the flag parses unchanged; with flag, spy on runAgenticLoop params. Build + typecheck.

principles:
# Project Principles

> These principles guide all planning, generation, and evaluation in the Bober pipeline.
> Updated: 2026-04-16

## Mission

Agent-bober is a multi-agent harness that builds applications autonomously using any LLM. It orchestrates a pipeline of specialized agents (Researcher, Planner, Curator, Generator, Evaluator) and supports Claude, GPT, Gemini, and Ollama. It ships as an npm CLI + MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, and Windsurf.

## Users

- **Primary:** Developers using Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf who want autonomous feature generation with quality gates
- **Secondary:** Teams integrating agent-bober programmatically via the TypeScript API or MCP server
- Technical level: intermediate to advanced — users understand LLMs, CI/CD, and modern JS tooling

## Quality Standards

- **Type safety:** TypeScript strict mode with all strict flags (`noUnusedLocals`, `noUnusedParameters`, `noImplicitReturns`, `noFallthroughCasesInSwitch`, `isolatedModules`). Zero type errors is a hard gate.
- **Lint compliance:** ESLint flat config with `consistent-type-imports` enforced, `no-explicit-any` warned, unused vars errored (with `_` prefix escape). Zero lint errors is a hard gate.
- **Test coverage:** Vitest for unit tests. Tests are collocated with source (`*.test.ts` next to `*.ts`). Tests run against the real project when practical (e.g., scanner tests scan agent-bober itself).
- **Build integrity:** `tsc` must produce clean output. Build is a required evaluator strategy.

## Technical Principles

### Follow

- **ESM everywhere.** `"type": "module"` in package.json. All imports use `.js` extensions for NodeNext resolution. No CommonJS.
- **Provider-agnostic interfaces.** All LLM interaction goes through `providers/types.ts` — provider-specific SDKs are wrapped by adapters (`anthropic.ts`, `openai.ts`, `google.ts`). Never leak SDK types outside adapter files.
- **Zod for config validation.** All configuration schemas are Zod schemas in `config/schema.ts`. Runtime config loading uses `z.parse()`. No hand-rolled validation.
- **Contract-based architecture.** PlanSpecs, SprintContracts, and EvalResults are the communication protocol between agents. They live in `contracts/` and are JSON files on disk in `.bober/`.
- **Filesystem state.** All mutable state (specs, contracts, handoffs, eval results, history) is stored as JSON files in `.bober/`. No database, no in-memory global state.
- **Section comments.** Use unicode box-drawing section headers: `// -- Section Name ------` to organize long files.
- **Small utility modules.** Utils in `utils/` are focused single-purpose files (`fs.ts`, `git.ts`, `logger.ts`). Keep them small.
- **Conventional commits.** Format: `type: description` where type is `feat`, `fix`, `docs`, `release`, `refactor`, `test`, `chore`. Sprint-generated commits use `bober(sprint-N): description`.
- **Use `type` imports.** ESLint enforces `consistent-type-imports` -- import types with `import type { ... }` syntax.
- **Prefix unused params with `_`.** The `_` prefix is the only escape hatch for unused variables/parameters.

### Avoid

- **No `any` without justification.** `no-explicit-any` is a warning; aim for zero. Use `unknown` + type narrowing instead.
- **No SDK lock-in.** Never import `@anthropic-ai/sdk` or `openai` outside of their respective adapter files in `providers/`.
- **No synchronous filesystem ops.** All fs operations use `node:fs/promises`. No `fs.readFileSync` etc.
- **No barrel re-exports for deep internals.** `src/index.ts` exports the public API only. Internal modules import directly.
- **No test mocks for filesystem.** Tests that need filesystem state create temp directories and clean up. The scanner tests run against the real codebase.

## Design Principles

N/A -- no user interface. Agent-bober is a CLI tool and programmatic library. The MCP server exposes tools, not UI.
